agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance, fraud prevention, and user trust are paramount.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that must be completed before a user is authorized to send money or withdraw funds."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has fully completed KYC. Permitted to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the daily transfer limit of $5,000."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account with incomplete KYC that has a reduced daily transfer limit of $500. The spec does not clarify what actions this tier can perform beyond having a limit."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account that has not begun or completed any KYC. Can receive money but cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement","definition":"The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation), after which the transaction becomes irreversible."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or jurisdiction appearing on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC). Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Only a support agent can unlock the account."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"The maximum cumulative amount a user can transfer in a single calendar day, varying by verification tier."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Users must complete full KYC identity verification before they are permitted to send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unverified users to send money violates regulatory requirements (AML/KYC). If this is bypassed, the system is in a non-compliant state.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to initiate a send-money transaction from an account with status other than 'verified' (or 'partially-verified' if that tier permits sends) must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw funds.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory compliance constraint. Permitting sends or withdrawals from unverified accounts creates legal and financial liability.","testable_assertion":"An unverified account must successfully receive an inbound transfer. Any send or withdraw request from an unverified account must be rejected with an appropriate error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit is $5,000 for verified accounts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The $5,000 threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted by product or compliance leadership without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a verified account within a calendar day must not exceed $5,000."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit is $500 for partially-verified accounts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The $500 threshold is a configurable business policy, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a partially-verified account within a calendar day must not exceed $500."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reversing a settled transaction would corrupt financial ledger integrity and break double-entry accounting invariants. Post-settlement corrections must go through a separate refund or dispute flow.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal request for a transaction whose settlement timestamp is in the past must be rejected. Only unsettled (pending) transactions may be reversed."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Processing a transfer to a sanctioned entity violates law (e.g., OFAC regulations). This is a hard legal constraint — if violated, the system and company are in legal jeopardy.","testable_assertion":"Any outbound transfer where the recipient matches a sanctioned entity list must be blocked before funds move, and an alert must be generated."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Five consecutive failed login attempts must lock the account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a security integrity constraint. If the system fails to lock after 5 consecutive failures, brute-force attacks can compromise accounts holding real money.","testable_assertion":"After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts on an account, the account status must transition to 'locked'. The 6th attempt must be rejected even with correct credentials."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Only a support agent can unlock a locked account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Self-service unlock would defeat the purpose of the security lock, potentially allowing an attacker who eventually guesses credentials to proceed. Human review is required for account integrity.","testable_assertion":"There must be no user-facing flow that transitions an account from 'locked' to 'active'. Only an authenticated support agent action can perform this transition."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The $1,000 notification threshold is a product policy that could be changed without corrupting system state. Missing a notification is a UX issue, not a data integrity issue.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is processed, a notification event must be emitted to the account holder."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"They attempt to send money","then":"The transaction is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"Another user sends them money","then":"The funds are credited to their account successfully"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"They attempt to withdraw funds","then":"The withdrawal is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified user who has already transferred $4,500 today","when":"They attempt to send $600","then":"The transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A verified user who has already transferred $4,500 today","when":"They attempt to send $500","then":"The transaction succeeds as the cumulative total ($5,000) equals the daily limit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A partially-verified user who has already transferred $400 today","when":"They attempt to send $200","then":"The transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction that was initiated yesterday and has now settled (T+1)","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is rejected with a message that settled transactions cannot be reversed"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A transaction that was initiated today and has not yet settled","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is processed successfully"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A verified user initiating a transfer","when":"The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"The transfer is blocked before funds move and a compliance alert is generated"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A user who has failed login 4 times consecutively","when":"They fail a 5th login attempt","then":"The account is locked and subsequent login attempts are rejected even with correct credentials"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A locked account","when":"The account holder attempts to log in with correct credentials","then":"Login is rejected and a message instructs them to contact support"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A locked account","when":"A support agent initiates an unlock","then":"The account is unlocked and the user can log in again"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A user who failed 4 times and then logs in successfully","when":"They subsequently fail once more","then":"The consecutive failure counter resets — the account is not locked (only 1 consecutive failure)"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A verified user sending a transaction of $1,500","when":"The transaction is processed","then":"A notification is sent to the account holder"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A verified user sending a transaction of $999","when":"The transaction is processed","then":"No high-value transaction notification is sent"},{"id":"AC-16","given":"A verified user sending exactly $1,000","when":"The transaction is processed","then":"No notification is sent, as the rule specifies 'over $1,000' (strictly greater than)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Partially-verified account attempts to send money","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — the spec says KYC is required to send, but also assigns a $500 limit to partially-verified accounts, implying they can send. This needs clarification (see escalation)."},{"scenario":"Daily limit boundary — user sends exactly $5,000 in one transaction","expected_behavior":"Transaction should succeed as it equals (does not exceed) the daily limit."},{"scenario":"Transaction amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"No notification sent — rule says 'over $1,000', meaning strictly greater than. Boundary should be documented clearly."},{"scenario":"User locked out, support unlocks, user immediately fails 5 more times","expected_behavior":"Account locks again — the lock mechanism applies independently each time the counter reaches 5 consecutive failures."},{"scenario":"Recipient is added to sanctions list after transfer initiated but before settlement","expected_behavior":"Not specified. The system should ideally re-screen at settlement, but this is not stated."},{"scenario":"Calendar day boundary — user transfers $4,000 at 11:59 PM and $4,000 at 12:01 AM","expected_behavior":"Both succeed, as they fall in different calendar days. The definition of 'day' (UTC vs. user timezone) needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Receiving money into a locked account","expected_behavior":"Not specified. Lock is described as a login security control — it likely should not block inbound receipts, but this needs confirmation."},{"scenario":"Partially-verified user receives a transfer of $2,000","expected_behavior":"Should succeed — receiving is not subject to daily transfer limits per the spec. Limits apply to outbound transfers."},{"scenario":"Transaction reversal requested on the same day as initiation (before T+1)","expected_behavior":"Should be permitted since settlement has not occurred, but the spec does not explicitly define a reversal window — only that post-settlement reversal is forbidden."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Direct revenue impact from transfer fees; daily limits cap transaction volume per user. Sanctions violations carry severe financial penalties (millions in fines). Incorrect limit enforcement could expose the platform to fraud losses.","operational":"KYC verification creates onboarding friction that affects conversion rates. Account locking requires support agent capacity for unlocks. T+1 settlement creates a reconciliation window that operations must manage.","user":"Tiered verification creates a progressive trust experience — users can receive money immediately but must verify to unlock full functionality. Account locking after 5 failures balances security with usability. Notifications for high-value transactions build trust.","risk":"Sanctions screening failure is an existential risk (license revocation, criminal liability). KYC bypass is a regulatory compliance risk. Settled transaction reversal is a financial integrity risk. Brute-force vulnerability from inadequate locking is a fraud risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users","impact":"Must complete KYC to access full functionality; experience account locks on failed logins; receive notifications for large transactions"},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"Depends on sanctions screening and KYC enforcement being airtight; liable for regulatory failures"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle account unlock requests; needs tooling and authority to unlock accounts"},{"group":"Finance/Treasury","impact":"T+1 settlement affects cash flow management; daily limits affect float and liquidity"},{"group":"Product/Growth","impact":"KYC friction affects user conversion; tiered limits affect user activation and engagement"}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The spec is mostly clear on the major rules, but contains one significant ambiguity around partially-verified accounts that creates conflicting interpretations of who can send money. The notification boundary ($1,000 threshold inclusive vs exclusive) is a minor ambiguity. No legal review has been performed on the sanctions screening or KYC requirements. The spec is silent on several edge cases (timezone for daily limits, sanctions list updates mid-transaction, behavior of locked accounts for inbound transfers).","high_confidence_areas":["Unverified accounts cannot send or withdraw","Settled transactions (T+1) are irreversible","Sanctions screening must block transfers","5 consecutive failed logins lock the account","Only support agents can unlock accounts"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partially-verified accounts can send money (contradicts KYC-required-to-send rule)","Whether $1,000 notification threshold is inclusive or exclusive","What timezone defines the 'daily' boundary for transfer limits","Whether locked accounts can still receive inbound transfers","Whether sanctions re-screening occurs at settlement","What constitutes a 'partially-verified' account vs unverified"],"assumptions":["Daily transfer limits apply to outbound transfers only, not inbound receipts","The $1,000 notification threshold is exclusive (strictly greater than) based on the word 'over'","A successful login resets the consecutive failure counter to zero","T+1 means one business day, not one calendar day, though the spec does not clarify","Sanctions screening happens at transaction initiation time, not at settlement"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states 'Users must complete identity verification (KYC) before they can send money' (implying only fully verified users can send), but also states 'Daily transfer limits are $500 for partially-verified accounts' (implying partially-verified users CAN send, just with lower limits). These two statements create an ambiguity: can partially-verified accounts send money or not? If they cannot send, the $500 limit is meaningless.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance/Legal Team","Product/Growth"],"options":["Partially-verified accounts CAN send up to $500/day — the KYC rule means 'at least partial verification is required to send'","Partially-verified accounts CANNOT send — the $500 limit applies to some other action (e.g., wallet-to-wallet within the platform vs. external transfers)","Partially-verified accounts can send domestically but not internationally, with the $500 limit applying to domestic sends"],"recommendation":"Option A is most likely the intent: partial verification unlocks sending with a $500 daily cap, full verification raises it to $5,000. This creates a progressive trust model that aligns with industry norms. Clarify with the PM."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define what 'daily' means for transfer limits — is it a rolling 24-hour window or a calendar day? If calendar day, what timezone? This materially affects limit enforcement for users near day boundaries.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance/Treasury","Engineering"],"options":["Calendar day in UTC","Calendar day in the user's local timezone","Rolling 24-hour window from first transaction"],"recommendation":"UTC calendar day is the industry standard for financial platforms and simplest to implement consistently. Recommend UTC with clear communication to users."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The notification rule says 'any transaction over $1,000' — it is ambiguous whether this applies only to sends, or also to receives, withdrawals, and other transaction types. Additionally, the boundary (exactly $1,000) is ambiguous — 'over' typically means strictly greater than, but financial systems often use >= for such thresholds.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Product/Growth"],"options":["Notification for sends only, strictly > $1,000","Notification for all transaction types (send, receive, withdraw), strictly > $1,000","Notification for all transaction types, >= $1,000"],"recommendation":"Notify on all transaction types >= $1,000. Users benefit from visibility on all large movements of their money, and using >= avoids the confusing edge case of exactly $1,000 receiving no notification."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

Missing a notification is a UX issue, not a data integrity issue.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is processed, a notification event must be emitted to the account The system should ideally re-screen at settlement, but this is not stated."},{"scenario":"Calendar day boundary — user transfers $4,000 at 11:59 PM and $4,000 at 12:01 AM","expected_behavior":"Both succeed, as they fall in different calendar days. Lock is described as a login security control — it likely should not block inbound receipts, but this needs confirmation."},{"scenario":"Partially-verified user receives a transfer of $2,000","expected_behavior":"Should succeed — receiving is not subject to daily transfer limits per the spec. Limits apply to outbound transfers."},{"scenario":"Transaction reversal requested on the same day as initiation (before T+1)","expected_behavior":"Should be permitted since settlement has not occurred, but the spec does not explicitly define a reversal window — only that post-settlement reversal is forbidden."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Direct revenue impact from transfer fees; daily limits cap transaction volume per user. Sanctions violations carry severe financial penalties (millions in fines). Account locking requires support agent capacity for unlocks. T+1 settlement creates a reconciliation window that operations must manage.","user":"Tiered verification creates a progressive trust experience — users can receive money immediately but must verify to unlock full functionality. Account locking after 5 failures balances security with usability. Notifications for high-value transactions build trust.","risk":"Sanctions screening failure is an existential risk (license revocation, criminal liability). KYC bypass is a regulatory compliance risk. Settled transaction reversal is a financial integrity risk. Brute-force vulnerability from inadequate locking is a fraud risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users","impact":"Must complete KYC to access full functionality; experience account locks on failed logins; receive notifications for large transactions"},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"Depends on sanctions screening and KYC enforcement being airtight; liable for regulatory failures"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle account unlock requests; needs tooling and authority to unlock accounts"},{"group":"Finance/Treasury","impact":"T+1 settlement affects cash flow management; daily limits affect float and liquidity"},{"group":"Product/Growth","impact":"KYC friction affects user conversion; tiered limits affect user activation and engagement"}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The spec is mostly clear on the major rules, but contains one significant ambiguity around partially-verified accounts that creates conflicting interpretations of who can send money. The spec is silent on several edge cases (timezone for daily limits, sanctions list updates mid-transaction, behavior of locked accounts for inbound transfers).","high_confidence_areas":["Unverified accounts cannot send or withdraw","Settled transactions (T+1) are irreversible","Sanctions screening must block transfers","5 consecutive failed logins lock the account","Only support agents can unlock accounts"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partially-verified accounts can send money (contradicts KYC-required-to-send rule)","Whether $1,000 notification threshold is inclusive or exclusive","What timezone defines the 'daily' boundary for transfer limits","Whether locked accounts can still receive inbound transfers","Whether sanctions re-screening occurs at settlement","What constitutes a 'partially-verified' account vs unverified"],"assumptions":["Daily transfer limits apply to outbound transfers only, not inbound receipts","The $1,000 notification threshold is exclusive (strictly greater than) based on the word 'over'","A successful login resets the consecutive failure counter to zero","T+1 means one business day, not one calendar day, though the spec does not clarify","Sanctions screening happens at transaction initiation time, not at settlement"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states 'Users must complete identity verification (KYC) before they can send money' (implying only fully verified users can send), but also states 'Daily transfer limits are $500 for partially-verified accounts' (implying partially-verified users CAN send, just with lower limits). The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance, fraud prevention, and user trust are paramount.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that must be completed before a user is authorized to send money or withdraw funds."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has fully completed KYC. Permitted to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the daily transfer limit of $5,000."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account with incomplete KYC that has a reduced daily transfer limit of $500. The spec does not clarify what actions this tier can perform beyond having a limit."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account that has not begun or completed any KYC. Can receive money but cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement","definition":"The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation), after which the transaction becomes irreversible."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or jurisdiction appearing on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC). Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
